name: Island Ice Cream Soda
recipeIngredient: 15 Ounce Can Cream of Coconut (e.g., Coco Lopez)
2 Tablespoons Dark Rum or 2 Teaspoons Vanilla Extract
2 In. Piece Ginger Peel, slice thinly
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the coconut ice cream:
a. In a heavy-duty electric mixer bowl set to medium-high speed, beat egg yolks and salt for 5-7 minutes until pale yellow and form a thick ribbon.
b. Heat cream in a saucepan over medium heat, then gradually stir into beaten egg yolks. Return mixture to the saucepan and cook gently stirring with a wooden spoon for 2-4 minutes until slightly thickened. It should leave a trail on your finger when pulled down the back of a spoon.
c. Remove pan from heat, strain through a sieve into a bowl over ice water to cool, then stir in cream of coconut and rum or vanilla extract. Refrigerate for at least 6 hours or overnight until very cold.
d. Transfer to an ice cream maker and freeze according to instructions. Cover surface with plastic wrap and tightly seal the container with aluminum foil. Freeze overnight.
2. Make the pineapple-ginger syrup:
a. Puree pineapple and ginger in food processor for 45 seconds. Transfer puree into saucepan, add sugar and cook over medium heat stirring constantly until boiling.
b. Simmer mixture for 10 minutes then cool completely. Strain through fine-mesh sieve, cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until chilled.
3. Assemble the ice cream sodas:
a. Beat heavy cream, sugar, and vanilla in mixer bowl at high speed until stiff peaks form. Refrigerate until ready to use.
b. Pour 1/2 cup pineapple syrup into glass, add seltzer water almost to top, place scoop of coconut ice cream on side, pipe rosette of whipped cream next to scoop, sprinkle with macadamia nuts and garnish with pineapple wedge. Repeat for 3 more sodas.
Serve immediately.
